title: Unit 10
description: The Please and Thank You Book

manners.title: 🙏 The Please and Thank You Book
manners.html:
Good manners are very important. When we want something, we say 'please'. When someone helps us, we say 'thank you'.

If someone is at the door, we knock before entering. If we want to borrow a pen, we say: 'Can I borrow your pen, please?'

It is rude to grab things from others. We should ask first! It is thoughtful to help others without them asking.

When someone gives you food, you can say: 'Please pass the salt.' If you bump into someone, you say: 'I'm sorry!' Being polite makes everyone feel happy.

'Can I go to the toilet?' asked Tom. 'May I go to the toilet?' is more polite. Both are correct, but 'May I...?' sounds more formal.

technology.title: 💻 Being Polite with Technology
technology.html:
We use technology every day. A computer or laptop helps us learn new things. A tablet is fun for reading and playing games.

We can use a camera to take photos. But we should always ask: 'Can I take your photo?' before we take a picture of someone.

A recorder can record sounds. A cell phone can do many things — call, text, take photos and play music!

Good manners with technology:

We should put away our phone when someone is talking to us. We should finish our work before playing games. We should share devices with our brothers and sisters.

'May I use the computer?' is a polite way to ask. Being respectful with technology is just as important as being respectful in person!
